Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses By Plaintiffs Gary And Jenifer Faw To Supplemental Interrogatories And Supplemental Request For Production Of Documents, Set 1; Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,185
Off Calendar Mar-03-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC'S Motion To Compel Responses By Plaintiffs Gary And Jenifer Faw To Supplemental Interrogatories And Supplemental Request For Production Of Documents, Set 1; Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,185. is granted, no opposition filed. Objection-free responses to discovery are due within 10 days of service of this order. Sanctions awarded against plaintiffs in the amount of $1,185, to be paid within 10 days of service of this order. Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Charles Geerhart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/JPT)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion
Off Calendar Feb-27-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MATTHEW D. HALEY of Haley Law Offices, APC, Tel No. (510) 444-1881 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. JUDY LIAO of Maranga Morgenstern, APC, Tel No. (415) 248-5315 appearing for Defendant United Parcel Service, Inc.

RULING - A R G U E D - DEFENDANT UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. First, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether UPS breached any duty to Plaintiffs. UPS concedes that, as the owner of the lift, it had a duty to ensure that the lift was in a safe and operable working condition. Plaintiff's testimony does not establish that UPS did not breach that duty. Additionally, Plaintiffs have produced unrebutted evidence that the lift was defective and inappropriate for the use authorized by UPS. (Smith Decl. at 4:4-7, 4:16-18.) Second, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether UPS's alleged negligence caused Plaintiffs' injuries. UPS argues only that there is insufficient direct evidence of causation, and that direct evidence cannot be obtained. However, Plaintiffs have produced unrebutted circumstantial evidence that their injuries were caused either by UPS's negligent authorization of use of the lift for the specific job, or by UPS's negligence in maintaining the lift in a safe and operable working condition. (Smith Decl. at 3-5.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Paula J. Shi, CSR #13358 (302/EHG)
